name: New Game Plus
description: A post-completion mode that starts a new playthrough while carrying selected account, character, item, difficulty, or completion state forward.
design_notes: New Game Plus is a migration problem disguised as a reward. The design should define carryover rules, reset rules, difficulty scaling, unlock eligibility, quest-state cleanup, duplicate reward prevention, and whether profile achievements can unlock retroactively in the new loop.
design_purpose: Reward completion by letting players replay with retained identity or power.
Extend mastery through harder encounters, alternate routes, or new rewards.
Create a controlled boundary for migrating save data into a repeat playthrough.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are beginning again with hard-earned mastery, gear, knowledge, or account status intact.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.typical_range: save slot or account profile id
parameters.description: Profile used as the source and destination for repeat playthrough state.

parameters.name: ng_plus_cycle_index
parameters.description: Repeat-playthrough cycle used for scaling and unlock rules.

parameters.name: carryover_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule describing which state is copied into the new cycle.

parameters.name: reset_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule describing which completion state returns to initial conditions.

parameters.name: difficulty_scaling_curve_id
parameters.description: Scaling profile for enemies, rewards, economy, or hazards.

parameters.name: migration_checkpoint_id
parameters.description: Rollback point used if playthrough migration fails.

parameters.name: duplicate_reward_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for rewards already owned when encountered again.

edge_cases: A quest item carried forward unlocks a progression gate before the world is ready.
A content update changes carryover rules for saves that already entered New Game Plus.
The migration fails after deleting old world state but before writing the new cycle.
Platform achievements depend on story flags that were reset for the new cycle.
A duplicated unique reward is granted again and corrupts inventory or economy balance.
common_bugs: Carryover copies item instances but not their upgrade, durability, or ownership metadata.
Quest flags reset visually while NPC or world scripts still read completed state.
Difficulty scaling stacks each load because ng_plus_cycle_index is reapplied repeatedly.
Map reveal, tutorial, or codex state is wiped even though the profile should retain it.
Rollback snapshots omit account_unlock_id records and lose permanent unlocks.
balancing_notes: Carryover power should justify replay without trivializing early progression unless that is the promise.
Rewards need duplicate policies before launch; repeated unique items become design debt quickly.
Scaling should account for carried tools and player knowledge, not only raw character stats.
accessibility_notes: Explain carryover and reset rules before the player commits to the new cycle.
Provide confirmation and backup handling for irreversible migration steps.
Avoid hiding new accessibility settings behind a harder repeat mode.

implementation_notes.unity: Create a pre-migration save snapshot, then process carryover_rule_id and reset_rule_id through a migration service instead of ad hoc scene scripts. Validate item instances, quest flags, map state, and account unlocks before replacing the active save.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent save data with versioned dictionaries or Resources and run New Game Plus migration as a single profile operation. Keep ng_plus_cycle_index, carried inventory ids, and reset quest flags explicit so old saves can migrate safely.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use SaveGame migration steps with named checkpoints and data validation. Gameplay Ability, inventory, and quest systems should rebuild from migrated state rather than retaining live actors from the completed playthrough.
implementation_notes.web: Treat New Game Plus as a profile transformation that writes a new save revision. In local or server storage, keep pre_migration and post_migration records long enough to recover from interrupted browser sessions or sync conflicts.

relationships.reason: New game plus extends long-term progression by carrying selected account or completion state into another playthrough.
New game plus persistence can blur run reset boundaries unless account and run state are separated.
Achievements can track completion milestones that unlock or recognize new game plus starts.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses New Game Plus cycles after completion with increased challenge.

example_games.title: Chrono Trigger
example_games.note: Popularized New Game Plus as a replay mode with retained progress.
